# Event studies

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Load required packages
required_packages <- c(
  "arrow", "here", "dplyr", "DIDmultiplegtDYN", "ggplot2",
  "future", "future.apply"
)

# Function to install missing packages
install_if_missing <- function(packages) {
  for (pkg in packages) {
    if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
      cat("Installing", pkg, "...\n")
      install.packages(pkg, lib = user_lib)
    }
    library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
  }
}

# Install and load packages
install_if_missing(required_packages)
```

## Monthly

### Binary

```{r}
# Set up parallel plan (adjust workers based on RAM and cores)
num_workers <- 3
plan(multisession, workers = num_workers)    

# message(paste("Setting up parallel execution using 'multicore' with", num_workers, "workers..."))
message(paste("Setting up parallel execution using 'multisession' with", num_workers, "workers..."))

# The rest of the script uses this plan via future_lapply

# Binary
# Function to run event study models with flexible parameters
run_bin_event_study <- function(param_grid, rad = 2000, overwrite = FALSE) {
  # Path for results files
  results_path <- here("book", "book_regs", paste0("binary_did_results_", rad, ".rds"))
  partial_path <- here("book", "book_regs", paste0("binary_did_results_partial_", rad, ".rds"))

  # Create directory if it doesn't exist
  results_dir <- dirname(results_path)
  if (!dir.exists(results_dir)) {
    dir.create(results_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    message("Created directory: ", results_dir)
  }

  # Load existing results if not overwriting and file exists
  existing_results <- list()
  if (!overwrite && file.exists(results_path)) {
    existing_results <- readRDS(results_path)
    message("Loaded existing results from: ", results_path)
  } else if (!overwrite) {
    message("No existing results file found or overwrite=TRUE. Starting fresh for radius ", rad)
  } else {
    message("overwrite=TRUE. Starting fresh for radius ", rad)
  }

  # Load the dataset for the current radius
  message(paste("Loading data for radius:", rad))
  dat_panel_site <- tryCatch({
    open_dataset(
      here::here("data", "processed", "dat_panel_site_mo")
    ) %>%
      filter(radius == rad) %>%
      collect()
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error loading data for radius ", rad, ": ", e$message)
    return(NULL) # Return NULL if data loading fails
  })

  if (is.null(dat_panel_site)) {
    message("Skipping radius ", rad, " due to data loading error.")
    return(existing_results) # Return existing (potentially empty) results
  }
  message(paste("Data loaded successfully for radius:", rad, " - Rows:", nrow(dat_panel_site)))

  # --- Helper function to run a single model ---
  run_single_bin_model <- function(params, data_for_radius) {
    # Extract parameters from the list 'params'
    outcome <- params$outcome
    treatment_threshold <- params$treatment_threshold
    time_agg <- params$time_agg
    normalized <- params$normalized
    same_switchers <- params$same_switchers
    treatment_type <- params$treatment_type

    # Construct the treatment variable name dynamically
    treatment_var <- paste0("treated_", treatment_type, "_", treatment_threshold, "_", time_agg)

    # Build model name
    model_name <- "model_bin"
    if (grepl("log_", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_logp")
    if (grepl("_w$", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_w")
    treat_type_short <- ifelse(treatment_type == "spill_count", "sc", "sh")
    model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", treat_type_short)
    model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", treatment_threshold)
    model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", time_agg)
    if (same_switchers) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_sw")
    if (normalized) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_norm")

    # Build model description
    outcome_label <- case_when(
      outcome == "price_median_w" ~ "Weighted Price Median",
      outcome == "log_price_median_w" ~ "Weighted Log Price Median",
      outcome == "price_median" ~ "Price Median",
      outcome == "log_price_median" ~ "Log Price Median",
      TRUE ~ outcome
    )
    threshold_label <- case_when(
      treatment_threshold == "mean" ~ "Mean",
      treatment_threshold == "p50" ~ "P50",
      treatment_threshold == "p75" ~ "P75",
      treatment_threshold == "p90" ~ "P90",
      treatment_threshold == "max" ~ "Max",
      TRUE ~ paste0(toupper(substr(treatment_threshold, 1, 1)), substr(treatment_threshold, 2, nchar(treatment_threshold)))
    )
    time_agg_label <- ifelse(time_agg == "mo", "Monthly", "Yearly")
    treat_type_label <- ifelse(treatment_type == "spill_count", "Spill Count", "Spill Hrs")
    norm_label <- ifelse(normalized, "Norm", "No Norm")
    switchers_label <- ifelse(same_switchers, "Same Switchers", "All Switchers")
    model_description <- sprintf(
      "%s ~ %s %s %s (%s, %s)",
      outcome_label, time_agg_label, threshold_label, treat_type_label,
      norm_label, switchers_label
    )

    cat(sprintf("Running model: %s (%s)\n", model_name, model_description))

    # Run the actual DID model
    model_result <- try(did_multiplegt_dyn(
      df = data_for_radius,
      outcome = outcome,
      group = "site_id",
      time = "month_id",
      treatment = treatment_var,
      effects = 12,
      placebo = 3,
      cluster = "site_id",
      normalized = normalized,
      trends_lin = FALSE,
      same_switchers = same_switchers,
      less_conservative_se = TRUE,
      graph_off = TRUE # Typically turn off graph in parallel runs
    ), silent = FALSE) # Set silent=FALSE to see errors from did_multiplegt_dyn

    # Return a named list for this model
    result <- list()
    result[[model_name]] <- model_result
    attr(result[[model_name]], "description") <- model_description
    return(result)
  }
  # --- End Helper function ---

  # Filter param_grid: Keep only models not present in existing_results if overwrite=FALSE
  models_to_run_indices <- 1:nrow(param_grid)
  if (!overwrite && length(existing_results) > 0) {
    existing_model_names <- names(existing_results)
    models_to_run_indices <- which(!apply(param_grid, 1, function(row) {
      # Reconstruct model name to check existence (ensure this logic matches exactly)
      outcome <- row[["outcome"]]
      treatment_threshold <- row[["treatment_threshold"]]
      time_agg <- row[["time_agg"]]
      normalized <- as.logical(row[["normalized"]]) # Ensure logical
      same_switchers <- as.logical(row[["same_switchers"]]) # Ensure logical
      treatment_type <- row[["treatment_type"]]

      model_name <- "model_bin"
      if (grepl("log_", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_logp")
      if (grepl("_w$", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_w")
      treat_type_short <- ifelse(treatment_type == "spill_count", "sc", "sh")
      model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", treat_type_short)
      model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", treatment_threshold)
      model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", time_agg)
      if (same_switchers) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_sw")
      if (normalized) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_norm")
      return(model_name %in% existing_model_names)
    }))

    if(length(models_to_run_indices) < nrow(param_grid)) {
      message(sprintf("Skipping %d models already present in results for radius %d.",
                      nrow(param_grid) - length(models_to_run_indices), rad))
    }
  }

  # Convert param_grid rows to a list of lists for lapply
  params_list <- split(param_grid[models_to_run_indices, , drop = FALSE], seq_len(length(models_to_run_indices)))

  total_models_to_run <- length(params_list)
  if (total_models_to_run == 0) {
    message("No new models to run for radius ", rad)
    return(existing_results)
  }

  cat(sprintf("Starting parallel model estimation for radius %d - total models to run: %d\n", rad, total_models_to_run))

  # Run models in parallel using future_lapply
  # Pass necessary objects explicitly if needed using future.globals
  # future_lapply automatically tries to find globals, but being explicit can help
  # Especially ensure 'dat_panel_site' and 'did_multiplegt_dyn' are available
  new_results_list <- future_lapply(params_list, run_single_bin_model,
                                   data_for_radius = dat_panel_site,
                                   future.seed = TRUE, # for reproducibility if needed
                                   future.globals = list(
                                     did_multiplegt_dyn = DIDmultiplegtDYN::did_multiplegt_dyn,
                                     case_when = dplyr::case_when
                                     # Add other necessary functions/objects if errors occur
                                   ),
                                   future.packages = c("dplyr", "DIDmultiplegtDYN") # Explicitly load packages on workers
  )

  # Combine the list of lists into a single named list
  new_results <- do.call(c, new_results_list)

  # Combine new results with existing ones
  # If overwrite=TRUE, existing_results is empty, so this just becomes new_results
  # If overwrite=FALSE, this adds new results to the loaded ones
  final_results <- c(existing_results, new_results)

  # Save the final results for this radius
  tryCatch({
    saveRDS(final_results, file = results_path)
    message("Results saved successfully for radius ", rad, " to: ", results_path)
    # Optionally remove partial file if main save succeeds
    if(file.exists(partial_path)) file.remove(partial_path)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error saving final results for radius ", rad, ": ", e$message)
    # Attempt to save partial results if final save fails
    tryCatch({
      saveRDS(final_results, file = partial_path)
      message("Partial results saved for radius ", rad, " to: ", partial_path)
    }, error = function(e_partial) {
      message("CRITICAL ERROR: Failed to save even partial results for radius ", rad, ": ", e_partial$message)
    })
  })

  cat("Finished model estimation for radius ", rad, "\n")

  # Return results invisibly
  invisible(final_results)
}

binary_param_grid <- expand.grid(
  outcome = c("log_price_median", "log_price_median_w"),
  treatment_threshold = c("mean", "p50", "p75", "p90", "max"),
  time_agg = c("mo", "yr"),
  treatment_type = c("spill_count", "spill_hrs"),
  normalized = c(TRUE),
  same_switchers = c(TRUE),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Define radii
radii <- c(2000, 1000, 500, 250)

# NEW sequential radii loop
bin_results_status <- list()
for (r in radii) {
  message(paste("\n--- Starting processing for radius:", r, "---\n"))
  success <- tryCatch({
    # Call the function for the current radius.
    # Set overwrite = FALSE to append/skip existing models by default
    # Set overwrite = TRUE if you want to rerun everything for this radius
    run_bin_event_study(binary_param_grid, rad = r, overwrite = FALSE)
    TRUE # Indicate success
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Error processing binary models for radius:", r, "-", e$message))
    FALSE # Indicate failure
  })
  bin_results_status[[as.character(r)]] <- success # Store status by radius
  # Optional: Add a small delay or GC call between radii if memory is an issue
  gc()
  Sys.sleep(1) # Small pause
}

message("Finished all binary event studies.")
# Check if any radius failed
if(any(!unlist(bin_results_status))) {
  failed_radii <- names(bin_results_status)[!unlist(bin_results_status)]
  warning("Binary event studies failed for radii: ", paste(failed_radii, collapse=", "), ". Check logs.")
} else {
  message("All binary event studies completed successfully for all radii.")
}

# Clean up memory before continuous runs
gc()
```

### Continuous

```{r}
# Continuous treatment
# Function to run event study models with flexible parameters
run_continuous_event_study <- function(param_grid, rad = 2000, overwrite = FALSE) {
  # Path for results files
  results_path <- here("book", "book_regs", paste0("cont_did_results_", rad, ".rds"))
  partial_path <- here("book", "book_regs", paste0("cont_did_results_partial_", rad, ".rds"))

  # Create directory if it doesn't exist
  results_dir <- dirname(results_path)
  if (!dir.exists(results_dir)) {
    dir.create(results_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    message("Created directory: ", results_dir)
  }

  # Load existing results if not overwriting and file exists
  existing_results <- list()
  if (!overwrite && file.exists(results_path)) {
    existing_results <- readRDS(results_path)
    message("Loaded existing continuous results from: ", results_path)
  } else if (!overwrite) {
    message("No existing continuous results file found or overwrite=TRUE. Starting fresh for radius ", rad)
  } else {
    message("overwrite=TRUE. Starting fresh for continuous models for radius ", rad)
  }

  # Load the dataset for the current radius
  message(paste("Loading data for continuous models, radius:", rad))
  dat_panel_site <- tryCatch({
    open_dataset(
      here::here("data", "processed", "dat_panel_site_mo")
    ) %>%
      filter(radius == rad) %>%
      collect()
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error loading data for continuous models, radius ", rad, ": ", e$message)
    return(NULL) # Return NULL if data loading fails
  })

  if (is.null(dat_panel_site)) {
    message("Skipping continuous models for radius ", rad, " due to data loading error.")
    return(existing_results) # Return existing (potentially empty) results
  }
  message(paste("Data loaded successfully for continuous models, radius:", rad, " - Rows:", nrow(dat_panel_site)))

  # --- Helper function to run a single continuous model ---
  run_single_cont_model <- function(params, data_for_radius) {
    # Extract parameters
    outcome <- params$outcome
    treatment_var <- params$treatment # Note: param name is 'treatment' in continuous_param_grid
    normalized <- params$normalized
    same_switchers <- params$same_switchers

    # Build model name
    model_name <- "model_cont"
    if (grepl("log_", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_logp")
    if (grepl("_w$", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_w")
    treat_short_name <- gsub("spill_count", "sc", treatment_var)
    treat_short_name <- gsub("spill_hrs", "sh", treat_short_name)
    treat_short_name <- gsub("log_", "log", treat_short_name) # Simplify log prefix if present
    model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", treat_short_name)
    # Add norm/sw flags (although fixed to TRUE in current grid)
    if (same_switchers) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_sw")
    if (normalized) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_norm")

    # Build model description
    outcome_label <- case_when(
      outcome == "price_median_w" ~ "Weighted Price Median",
      outcome == "log_price_median_w" ~ "Weighted Log Price Median",
      outcome == "price_median" ~ "Price Median",
      outcome == "log_price_median" ~ "Log Price Median",
      TRUE ~ outcome
    )
    treatment_label <- case_when(
      treatment_var == "spill_count" ~ "Spill Count",
      treatment_var == "log_spill_count" ~ "Log Spill Count",
      treatment_var == "spill_hrs" ~ "Spill Hours",
      treatment_var == "log_spill_hrs" ~ "Log Spill Hours",
      TRUE ~ treatment_var
    )
    norm_label <- ifelse(normalized, "Norm", "No Norm")
    switchers_label <- ifelse(same_switchers, "Same Switchers", "All Switchers")
    model_description <- sprintf(
      "%s ~ %s (Continuous; %s, %s)",
      outcome_label, treatment_label, norm_label, switchers_label
    )

    cat(sprintf("Running model: %s (%s)\n", model_name, model_description))

    # Run the actual DID model for continuous treatment
    model_result <- try(did_multiplegt_dyn(
      df = data_for_radius,
      outcome = outcome,
      group = "site_id",
      time = "month_id",
      treatment = treatment_var,
      effects = 9,
      placebo = 2,
      cluster = "site_id",
      normalized = normalized,
      trends_lin = FALSE,
      same_switchers = same_switchers,
      less_conservative_se = TRUE,
      graph_off = TRUE, # Turn off graph in parallel runs
      effects_equal = TRUE # Specific to continuous setup
    ), silent = FALSE) # Keep silent = FALSE to see errors

    # Return a named list for this model
    result <- list()
    result[[model_name]] <- model_result
    attr(result[[model_name]], "description") <- model_description
    return(result)
  }
  # --- End Helper function ---

  # Filter param_grid: Keep only models not present in existing_results if overwrite=FALSE
  models_to_run_indices <- 1:nrow(param_grid)
  if (!overwrite && length(existing_results) > 0) {
    existing_model_names <- names(existing_results)
    models_to_run_indices <- which(!apply(param_grid, 1, function(row) {
      # Reconstruct model name to check existence
      outcome <- row[["outcome"]]
      treatment_var <- row[["treatment"]]
      normalized <- as.logical(row[["normalized"]])
      same_switchers <- as.logical(row[["same_switchers"]])

      model_name <- "model_cont"
      if (grepl("log_", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_logp")
      if (grepl("_w$", outcome)) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_w")
      treat_short_name <- gsub("spill_count", "sc", treatment_var)
      treat_short_name <- gsub("spill_hrs", "sh", treat_short_name)
      treat_short_name <- gsub("log_", "log", treat_short_name)
      model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_", treat_short_name)
      if (same_switchers) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_sw")
      if (normalized) model_name <- paste0(model_name, "_norm")
      return(model_name %in% existing_model_names)
    }))

    if(length(models_to_run_indices) < nrow(param_grid)) {
      message(sprintf("Skipping %d continuous models already present in results for radius %d.",
                      nrow(param_grid) - length(models_to_run_indices), rad))
    }
  }

  # Convert param_grid rows to a list of lists for lapply
  params_list <- split(param_grid[models_to_run_indices, , drop = FALSE], seq_len(length(models_to_run_indices)))

  total_models_to_run <- length(params_list)
  if (total_models_to_run == 0) {
    message("No new continuous models to run for radius ", rad)
    return(existing_results)
  }

  cat(sprintf("Starting parallel continuous model estimation for radius %d - total models to run: %d\n", rad, total_models_to_run))

  # Run models in parallel using future_lapply
  new_results_list <- future_lapply(params_list, run_single_cont_model,
                                     data_for_radius = dat_panel_site,
                                     future.seed = TRUE,
                                     future.globals = list(
                                         did_multiplegt_dyn = DIDmultiplegtDYN::did_multiplegt_dyn,
                                         case_when = dplyr::case_when
                                     ),
                                     future.packages = c("dplyr", "DIDmultiplegtDYN")
                                     )

  # Combine the list of lists into a single named list
  new_results <- do.call(c, new_results_list)

  # Combine new results with existing ones
  final_results <- c(existing_results, new_results)

  # Save the final results for this radius
  tryCatch({
    saveRDS(final_results, file = results_path)
    message("Continuous results saved successfully for radius ", rad, " to: ", results_path)
    if(file.exists(partial_path)) file.remove(partial_path)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error saving final continuous results for radius ", rad, ": ", e$message)
    # Attempt to save partial results if final save fails
    tryCatch({
      saveRDS(final_results, file = partial_path)
      message("Partial continuous results saved for radius ", rad, " to: ", partial_path)
    }, error = function(e_partial) {
      message("CRITICAL ERROR: Failed to save even partial continuous results for radius ", rad, ": ", e_partial$message)
    })
  })

  cat("Finished continuous model estimation for radius ", rad, "\n")

  # Return results invisibly
  invisible(final_results)
}

continuous_param_grid <- expand.grid(
  outcome = c("log_price_median", "log_price_median_w"),
  treatment = c("spill_count", "log_spill_count", "spill_hrs", "log_spill_hrs"),
  normalized = c(TRUE),
  same_switchers = c(TRUE),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# NEW sequential radii loop
cont_results_status <- list()
for (r in radii) {
  message(paste("\n--- Starting continuous processing for radius:", r, "---\n"))
  success <- tryCatch({
    # Call the function for the current radius.
    # Set overwrite = FALSE to append/skip existing models by default
    run_continuous_event_study(continuous_param_grid, rad = r, overwrite = FALSE)
    TRUE # Indicate success
  }, error = function(e) {
    message(paste("Error processing continuous models for radius:", r, "-", e$message))
    FALSE # Indicate failure
  })
  cont_results_status[[as.character(r)]] <- success # Store status by radius
  # Optional: Add GC call and sleep between radii
  gc()
  Sys.sleep(1)
}

message("Finished all continuous event studies.")
# Check if any radius failed
if(any(!unlist(cont_results_status))) {
  failed_radii <- names(cont_results_status)[!unlist(cont_results_status)]
  warning("Continuous event studies failed for radii: ", paste(failed_radii, collapse=", "), ". Check logs.")
} else {
    message("All continuous event studies completed successfully for all radii.")
}

# Reset to sequential plan after parallel execution (good practice)
plan(sequential)
```




## Quarterly

### Binary

```{r}
# 1. GRID & PARALLEL SETUP ---------------------------------------------------

num_workers <- 2
plan(multisession, workers = num_workers)
message("Parallelising with multisession × ", num_workers, " workers")

param_grid <- expand.grid(
  outcome             = c("log_price_median", "log_price_median_w"),
  treatment_threshold = c("mean", "p50", "p75", "p90"),
  time_agg            = c("qt", "yr"),
  treatment_type      = c("spill_count", "spill_hrs"),
  normalized          = TRUE,
  same_switchers      = TRUE,
  stringsAsFactors    = FALSE
)

# 2. SINGLE‐RADIUS FUNCTION --------------------------------------------------

run_radius <- function(rad, params, overwrite = FALSE) {
  
  # Paths
  base    <- here("book", "book_regs")
  out_fn  <- sprintf("binary_did_results_%d.rds", rad)
  part_fn <- sprintf("binary_did_results_partial_%d.rds", rad)
  results_path <- file.path(base, out_fn)
  partial_path <- file.path(base, part_fn)
  dir.create(base, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  
  # Load or init
  existing <- if (!overwrite && file.exists(results_path)) readRDS(results_path) else list()
  
  # Load data
  message("→ Loading radius ", rad)
  df <- tryCatch(
    open_dataset(here("data", "processed", "dat_panel_site_qtr")) %>%
      filter(radius == rad) %>% collect(),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(df)) {
    message("✗ Failed to load data for ", rad); return(existing)
  }
  message("✔ Loaded ", nrow(df), " rows for radius ", rad)
  
  # Build a unique model name
  make_model_name <- function(p) {
    parts <- c(
      "model_bin",
      if (grepl("log_", p$outcome)) "_log" else "",
      if (grepl("_w$",  p$outcome)) "_w"   else "",
      if (p$treatment_type == "spill_count") "_sc" else "_sh",
      "_", p$treatment_threshold,
      "_", p$time_agg,
      if (p$same_switchers) "_sw" else "",
      if (p$normalized)     "_norm" else ""
    )
    paste0(parts, collapse = "")
  }
  
  # Which to run?
  all_names <- vapply(split(params, seq(nrow(params))), make_model_name, "")
  to_run <- setdiff(all_names, names(existing))
  if (!overwrite && length(to_run) == 0) {
    message("Nothing new to run for radius ", rad)
    return(existing)
  }
  
  # Single‐model runner
  run_one <- function(p) {
    nm <- make_model_name(p)
    treat_var <- sprintf("treated_%s_%s_%s",
                         p$treatment_type, p$treatment_threshold, p$time_agg)
    cat("• Running", nm, "\n")
    res <- did_multiplegt_dyn(
      df                   = df,
      outcome              = p$outcome,
      group                = "site_id",
      time                 = "qtr_id",
      treatment            = treat_var,
      effects              = 5,
      placebo              = 1,
      cluster              = "site_id",
      normalized           = p$normalized,
      same_switchers       = p$same_switchers,
      less_conservative_se = TRUE,
      trends_lin           = FALSE,
      graph_off            = TRUE
    )
    attr(res, "description") <- nm
    setNames(list(res), nm)
  }
  
  # Parallel mapping
  new_results <- params %>%
    split(seq(nrow(.))) %>%
    keep(~ make_model_name(.) %in% to_run) %>%
    future_map(run_one, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)) %>%
    flatten()
  
  final <- c(existing, new_results)
  
  # Save
  tryCatch({
    saveRDS(final, results_path)
    if (file.exists(partial_path)) file.remove(partial_path)
    message("✔ Saved full results for ", rad)
  }, error = function(e) {
    saveRDS(final, partial_path)
    warning("⚠ Saved partial to ", partial_path)
  })
  
  invisible(final)
}

# 3. MULTI‐RADIUS LOOP -------------------------------------------------------

radii <- c(2000, 1000, 500, 250)
statuses <- setNames(logical(length(radii)), radii)

for (r in radii) {
  message("\n── Processing radius ", r, " ──")
  ok <- tryCatch({
    run_radius(r, param_grid, overwrite = TRUE)
    TRUE
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("✗ Error at ", r, ": ", e$message)
    FALSE
  })
  statuses[as.character(r)] <- ok
  gc(); Sys.sleep(1)
}

if (any(!statuses)) {
  warning("Failed radii: ", paste(names(statuses)[!statuses], collapse = ", "))
} else {
  message("All radii completed successfully.")
}

```

### Continuous

```{r}
# 1. GRID & PARALLEL SETUP ---------------------------------------------------
num_workers <- 2
plan(multisession, workers = num_workers)
message("Parallelising continuous event studies with multisession × ", num_workers, " workers")

continuous_param_grid <- expand.grid(
  outcome        = c("log_price_median", "log_price_median_w"),
  treatment      = c("spill_count", "log_spill_count", "spill_hrs", "log_spill_hrs"),
  normalized     = TRUE,
  same_switchers = TRUE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# 2. SINGLE‐RADIUS FUNCTION --------------------------------------------------
run_radius_cont <- function(rad, params, overwrite = FALSE) {
  
  # Paths
  base         <- here("book", "book_regs")
  results_path <- file.path(base, sprintf("cont_did_results_%d.rds", rad))
  partial_path <- file.path(base, sprintf("cont_did_results_partial_%d.rds", rad))
  dir.create(base, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  
  # Existing results
  existing <- if (!overwrite && file.exists(results_path)) {
    message("Loaded existing continuous results for radius ", rad)
    readRDS(results_path)
  } else {
    list()
  }
  
  # Load data
  message("→ Loading data for continuous models at radius ", rad)
  df <- tryCatch(
    open_dataset(here("data", "processed", "dat_panel_site_qtr")) %>%
      filter(radius == rad) %>%
      collect(),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (is.null(df)) {
    message("✗ Cannot load data for radius ", rad, "; skipping.")
    return(existing)
  }
  message("✔ Data loaded: ", nrow(df), " rows")
  
  # Helper to build model names
  make_cont_name <- function(p) {
    short_trt <- p$treatment %>%
      gsub("spill_count", "sc", .) %>%
      gsub("spill_hrs",  "sh", .) %>%
      gsub("^log_",      "log", .)
    
    parts <- c(
      "model_cont",
      if (grepl("log_", p$outcome)) "_log" else "",
      if (grepl("_w$",  p$outcome))  "_w"   else "",
      "_", short_trt,
      if (p$same_switchers) "_sw"   else "",
      if (p$normalized)     "_norm" else ""
    )
    paste0(parts, collapse = "")
  }
  
  # Figure out which to run
  all_names <- vapply(split(params, seq(nrow(params))), make_cont_name, "")
  to_run   <- if (overwrite) all_names else setdiff(all_names, names(existing))
  if (length(to_run) == 0) {
    message("Nothing new to run for radius ", rad)
    return(existing)
  }
  message(length(to_run), " continuous models queued for radius ", rad)
  
  # Single‐model runner
  run_one_cont <- function(p) {
    nm <- make_cont_name(p)
    cat("• Running ", nm, "\n", sep = "")
    
    res <- did_multiplegt_dyn(
      df                   = df,
      outcome              = p$outcome,
      group                = "site_id",
      time                 = "qtr_id",
      treatment            = p$treatment,
      effects              = 5,
      placebo              = 1,
      cluster              = "site_id",
      normalized           = p$normalized,
      trends_lin           = FALSE,
      same_switchers       = p$same_switchers,
      less_conservative_se = TRUE,
      graph_off            = TRUE,
      effects_equal        = TRUE
    )
    setNames(list(res), nm)
  }
  
  # Parallel execution
  new_results <- params %>%
    split(seq(nrow(.))) %>%
    keep(~ make_cont_name(.) %in% to_run) %>%
    future_map(run_one_cont, .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)) %>%
    flatten()
  
  final <- c(existing, new_results)
  
  # Save with partial‐on‐error fallback
  tryCatch({
    saveRDS(final, results_path)
    if (file.exists(partial_path)) file.remove(partial_path)
    message("✔ Saved continuous results for radius ", rad)
  }, error = function(e) {
    saveRDS(final, partial_path)
    warning("⚠ Couldn’t write full file. Saved partial to ", partial_path)
  })
  
  invisible(final)
}

# 3. MULTI‐RADIUS LOOP -------------------------------------------------------
radii <- c(2000, 1000, 500, 250)
status <- setNames(logical(length(radii)), radii)

for (r in radii) {
  message("\n── Continuous radius ", r, " ──")
  success <- tryCatch({
    run_radius_cont(r, continuous_param_grid, overwrite = FALSE)
    TRUE
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("✗ Radius ", r, " failed: ", e$message)
    FALSE
  })
  status[as.character(r)] <- success
  gc(); Sys.sleep(1)
}

if (any(!status)) {
  warning("Continuous studies failed for radii: ", paste(names(status)[!status], collapse = ", "))
} else {
  message("All continuous event studies completed successfully.")
}

plan(sequential)  
```
